---
title: "Data mining"
output: html_document
date: "2025-12-16"
---

```{r}

library(huge)
library(qgraph)
library(igraph)
library(dplyr)

```

```{r}

df <- read.csv("df_test.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

```


Analyse des datas

```{r}


summary(df)


```

```{r}
# pour avoir les ecart types

library(tidyverse)

df <- read_csv("df_test.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

sapply(df %>% select(where(is.numeric), -year),
       sd, na.rm = TRUE)



```

```{r}

vars_plot <- c("GDP growth (annual %)",
               "Inflation, consumer prices (annual %)")

df_plot <- df %>%
  select(all_of(vars_plot)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to = "variable",
               values_to = "value")

ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = variable, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribution de la croissance du PIB et de l'inflation",
       x = NULL, y = NULL)


```


```{r}

ggsave("boxplots_growth_inflation.png",
       width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)

```




```{r}



# 1) On enlève les colonnes qui ne sont pas des variables du modèle (ID / index)
X <- df[, !(names(df) %in% c("Country", "year", "X", "Unnamed: 0"))]

# 2) On enlève les lignes avec NA (si tu en as)
X <- na.omit(X)

# 3) On standardise (centrer-réduire)
X <- scale(X)

# Vérifs rapides
dim(X)          # doit donner n x p
colMeans(X)     # ~ 0
apply(X, 2, sd) # ~ 1



```


```{r}

n_distinct(df$Country)


```
GGM

```{r}


fit <- huge(X, method = "glasso")
sel <- huge.select(fit, criterion = "ebic")
adj <- sel$refit



```

```{r}


library(qgraph)

qgraph(adj,
       layout = "spring",
       labels = colnames(X),
       title = "GGM (Graphical Lasso) – Dépendances conditionnelles")


```



#test final pour avoir les bons noms 


```{r}


library(tidyverse)
library(huge)
library(qgraph)

# 1) Charger les données
df <- read_csv("df_har.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

```


```{r}



# 2) Garder uniquement les variables numériques utiles
X <- df %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%   # uniquement numérique
  select(-any_of(c("year", "X", "...1"))) %>%  # enlève index / année
  drop_na()                       # enlève NA



```



```{r}


# 1) Dictionnaire : ancien nom -> nouveau label
rename_map <- c(
  "GDP growth (annual %)" = "GDP_growth",
  "Inflation, consumer prices (annual %)" = "Inflation",
  "GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)" = "GDP_pc",
  "Total natural resources rents (% of GDP)" = "Resources",
  "Gross capital formation (% of GDP)" = "Investment",
  "Industry (including construction), value added (% of GDP)" = "Industry",
  "Gross national expenditure (% of GDP)" = "Expenditure",
  "Merchandise trade (% of GDP)" = "Trade",
  "Government Effectiveness: Estimate" = "Gov_eff",
  "Foreign direct investment, net outflows (% of GDP)" = "FDI"
)

# 2) Appliquer le renommage uniquement si la colonne existe
present <- intersect(names(rename_map), colnames(X))
colnames(X)[match(present, colnames(X))] <- rename_map[present]

# 3) Conversion finale en matrice (si tu utilises huge)
X <- as.matrix(X)


```



```{r}

# 3) Scaling + conversion en matrice
X <- as.matrix(X)

# GGM via Graphical Lasso
fit <- huge(X, method = "glasso")
```

```{r}


# 5) Sélection du lambda
sel <- huge.select(fit, criterion = "ebic")

# 6) Graphe
qgraph(sel$refit,
       labels = colnames(X),
       title = "Gaussian Graphical Model (Graphical Lasso)")


```


```{r}

# Afficher la matrice complète (attention: peut être long)
print(round(Pcor, 3))

# Afficher seulement les 15 plus fortes corrélations partielles (hors diagonale)
pcor_top <- as.data.frame(as.table(Pcor)) %>%
  filter(Var1 != Var2) %>%
  mutate(abs_val = abs(Freq)) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs_val)) %>%
  distinct(pmin(as.character(Var1), as.character(Var2)),
           pmax(as.character(Var1), as.character(Var2)),
           .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(var1 = Var1, var2 = Var2, pcor = Freq, abs_pcor = abs_val)

print(head(pcor_top, 15))


```
